summary: Cyclophosphamide does not commonly lead to nail pigmentation based on available literature; instead, medications such as chloroquine are more frequently associated with this effect. Therefore, cyclophosphamide stands out as less likely to cause nail pigmentation among given choices. The primary focus here was identifying which medication has documented cases versus those without significant reports linking them directly to nail color change issues.
